actionLibraryList.title: Tabletop Thoracic Rotation
actionLibraryList.context: During the rotation, avoid rushing the movement; maintain slow, controlled motion to prevent overstretching or misalignment.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523622522.jpg","context":"1.Raise one arm with the elbow bent and place your hand behind your head."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523625921.jpg","context":"1.During the rotation, keep your hand gently touching or lightly supporting your head.\n2.Keep your pelvis and hips stable without moving.\n3.Position your knees hip-width apart."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during the rotation to help relax your body.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: At the maximum range, hold for one to two seconds, feel the stretch in your thoracic spine, and keep your chest open.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: During thoracic spine rotation, keep your hips from rotating.

actionLibraryList.title: Hip-Opening Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Hold the machine at your sides and slowly lower into a deep squat, keeping your back straight (neutral spine).
2.In the deep squat, slowly shift your weight from one foot to the other.
3.This movement can effectively activate the relevant muscles before performing the squat.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Grip the machine with both hands and slowly lower yourself into a squat.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Use your arms for support while moving your hips.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Maintain an upright posture with your back.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: This exercise can effectively activate your body before performing squats.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Hold the sides of the machine with both hands and perform a deep squat.\n2.Gradually shift your weight onto each foot.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523929609.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This exercise can effectively activate your body before performing squats.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Maintain an upright posture with your back.

actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (bent arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand in the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean forward slightly with your hands gripping the handles.
2.Keep your arms bent, initiating the pull by engaging your core. Swing your arms back past your thighs.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the slider to the highest position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at your hips with a slight bend in your knees, and grip the handles with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Knees bend and extend in a fluid motion, while your hips sit back and down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down to your hips, engage your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.\n2.Hold the ski handles with both hands, keeping them no higher than shoulder level.\n3.Bend your elbows and pull the ropes down to your sides with force.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671815181.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your arms close to your body and maintain good balance throughout.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671818931.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale while pulling, inhale when returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down toward your hips, activate your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.

actionLibraryList.title: Belt Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Center your weight over the middle of your feet.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the rope exit position to the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Squat until your thighs are parallel to or slightly lower than the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your chin up, chest out, back straight, core tight, and toes in line with your knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Inhale as you lower, and exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate the upward movement by engaging your glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750670085751.jpg","context":"1.Secure the belt just above your hips."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750670089784.jpg","context":"1.Keep your back straight.\n2.Squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement activates the quadriceps and glutes.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Lift your head and chest, avoid arching your back, engage your core, and keep your toes and knees aligned.

actionLibraryList.title: Incline Dual-Handle Front Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie face-up on an incline bench (30-45 degrees). 
2.Grip a handle in each hand, palms down. 
3.Engage your core, keep your shoulder blades stable, and inhale to prepare.
4.Exhale as you raise your arms forward and upward until they form a 90-degree angle with your torso.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748228177922.jpg","context":"1.Grip a handle in each hand by your sides, with your palms facing down."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748228180258.jpg","context":"1.Exhale as you extend both arms forward and upward until they form a 90-degree angle with your body."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you push, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Coordinated effort between the anterior deltoids and upper chest.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid shrugging your shoulders, locking your elbows, or using momentum to lift the arms.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Bent-Over Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged.
2.Hinge forward 45°–60°, keeping a neutral spine.
3.Grip the handles with palms facing each other, arms hanging naturally.
4.Pull the handles up along your sides to your ribs or lower chest.
5.Pause briefly at the top, then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the floor with two handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Bend at the waist until your torso forms a forty five degree angle with your hips and slightly bend your knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Engage your core to stabilize yourself.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your chin up and spine extended, elbows close to your sides.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep breathing. Exhale on your way up and inhale on your way down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Pull the handles down toward your chest by engaging your lats. Slightly hold the contraction at the top.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Depress your shoulders.\n2.Hinge your upper body forward.\n3.Keep your knees slightly bent.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749002087858.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your elbows close to your sides.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749002091215.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pulling, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your upper arms close to your sides, head up, and spine extended.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Straight-Arm Glute Bridge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent.
2.Hold a handle in each hand, arms straight at your sides.
3.Engage your core, keeping your back and glutes close to the bench.
4.Lift your hips until your thighs and torso form a straight line.
5.Pause at the top, then slowly lower your hips back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749091873197.jpg","context":"1.Keep your arms straight at your sides."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749091877068.jpg","context":"1.Lift your hips until your thighs and torso form a straight line."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid using your lower back, and keep your knees aligned with your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Alternating V-Ups
actionLibraryList.context: Lie on your back with legs straight. Exhale as you lift your upper body, reaching one hand toward the opposite foot. Inhale as you lower back down. Alternate sides, keeping your core tight and lower back pressed to the floor. Maintain a smooth, controlled rhythm, avoiding jerking or straining your neck forward.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750047755511.jpg","context":"1.Lie on your back with legs straight."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750047760124.jpg","context":"1.Exhale as you lift your upper body, reaching one hand toward the opposite foot."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you contract, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid using momentum or straining your neck forward.
